rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE content, with type scopes for constants and equations, and implementation scopes for assignments.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE expressions use only visible identifiers and preserve type consistency.
Avoid introducing package-qualified names or types not present in the model.
Write stateful equations with initialized temporal recurrence and use nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` with correct AGREE syntax and types.
Place component type contracts in component type annexes; implementation contracts in implementation annexes.
Use `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>` for logical expressions.
Define helper functions only when they are available in the model or imported packages.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed.
Do not attempt to convert Boolean domain values into integers unless scalar representation is confirmed.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair decisions
Model unit and package dependency staging
